The proposed approach extends the idea of hybrid representation by storing the weights of an MLP on a multi-resolution tiled grid.
The weights of an MLP are stored on a multi-resolution tiled grid.
For each of the linear layers, multiple independent copies of its weights are arranged in a tiling pattern on the multi-resolution tiled grid.
The weights of the MLP become position-dependent when partitioning the space so that each copy of weights only needs to handle inputs within specific intervals.
The proposed framework, Levels-of-Experts (LoE), has layers at different depths, each with a number of experts specialized in different partitions of the input space.
Similar to Fourier features, different grid resolutions are used for each layer, so the weight of different layers repeats at different frequencies.
While the weight of each layer is repeating, there is a learnable combined parameterization achieved.
The learnable combined parameterization of all the layers helps avoid repetition over the input range of interest.
A large number of uniquely parameterized intervals of the combined model can be obtained and tailored to the underlying problem.
The position-dependent weight can itself serve as a form of positional encoding.

node_2: various other approaches of improving or extending implicit neural representations (inrs)
edge: The model discussed is orthogonal to various approaches aimed at enhancing or expanding implicit neural representations (INRs) like better activation functions, input encoding, hypernetworks, or combination with pre- or post-processing CNNs. It focuses on enabling higher fidelity data synthesis, representation, and compression at lower computational costs.

The method introduced in the context can lead to higher fidelity data synthesis, representation, and compression with reduced computational expenses when compared to previous works. However, it also acknowledges the possibility of misuse for negative purposes like creating deepfakes.
